county_metadata.record_losses: 1864 courthouse burned during Civil War guerrilla raid; most records destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Columbia. 1864 guerrilla raid burned courthouse — most pre-war records destroyed. KDLA has some microfilmed copies of surviving records. Adair was a parent county for Metcalfe and Russell. Green River region — check Virginia military bounty warrants for early settlers south of the river. Columbia Adair County Public Library has genealogy room. Strong Baptist and Christian church records supplement lost courthouse records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1864 to present. Some gaps due to 1864 courthouse burned during Civil War guerrilla raid; most records destroyed.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1864 courthouse burned during Civil War guerrilla raid; most records destroyed

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1864. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1864 courthouse burned during Civil War guerrilla raid; most records destroyed

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists often survive when other records are lost — many at KDLA on microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Scottsville. Records intact from 1815 formation. Allen County Historical Society active. Scottsville sits near the Tennessee line — cross-border families common with Macon County, TN. Allen County Public Library has genealogy collection. Barren River area. Check Barren and Warren counties for pre-1815 ancestors. FamilySearch microfilm available for deeds and marriages.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1815 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1815. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1815.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lawrenceburg. Formed 1827 from Franklin, Mercer, and Washington — check all three parent counties for earlier records. Wild Turkey and Four Roses distilleries document worker families. Anderson County Historical Society. Salt River heritage. Kentucky River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. Bourbon distillery employment records are distinctive local sources.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1827 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1827. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Wickliffe. Formed 1842 from Hickman and McCracken. Far western Kentucky (Jackson Purchase region, acquired from Chickasaw Nation 1818). Wickliffe Mounds archaeological site documents pre-contact Native American presence. Ohio and Mississippi River confluence area — river trade records. Check McCracken County for pre-1842 records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1842 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1842. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1842.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Glasgow. Records well-preserved from 1798. Major Scots-Irish settlement area via the Wilderness Road. South-central KY hub — parent county for several others. Glasgow is a regional center with city directories from 1890s. Barren County Historical Society. Mammoth Cave area (northern Barren County). Strong Baptist, Methodist, and Presbyterian church records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Green and Warren counties for pre-1798 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Owingsville. 1844 fire destroyed many early records — check KDLA for surviving microfilm. Formed 1811 from Montgomery. Named for medicinal springs (Olympian Springs). Eastern Kentucky foothills. Bath County Historical Society. Owingsville has iron furnace heritage — iron industry worker records at KY Historical Society. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1844 to present. Some gaps due to 1844 fire destroyed many early records.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1844. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Pineville. Formed 1867 from Knox and Harlan. Cumberland Gap is in Bell County — the primary passage for westward migration (1775-1810). Pine Mountain settlement pattern. Coal mining began 1880s — company town records, mine payrolls, and union records at Southeast Community College archives. Cumberland Gap National Historical Park archives. Bell County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1867 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1867. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1867.

source_profiles.source_name: Bell County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Burlington. Formed 1798 from Campbell. Named for Daniel Boone, who lived in the county. Northern Kentucky (Greater Cincinnati metro area). Boone County Public Library local history collection is excellent. Big Bone Lick State Historic Site. Swiss and German immigrant families in Rabbit Hash and other river communities. FamilySearch microfilm. Strong Catholic church records from German settlers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Paris. One of Kentucky's original nine counties from Virginia era (formed 1785). Named for French royal house — reflects Virginia's gratitude for French aid in Revolution. Paris is Bluegrass horse country center. Bourbon County records at KDLA from 1786. Hopewell Museum has local genealogy. Thoroughbred horse farm records document worker families. Cane Ridge Meeting House (1801 Revival) records. Check Fayette County (VA era) for pre-1785 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1785 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1785. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Land Grants — Bourbon County Area
source_profiles.repository: Library of Virginia, Richmond / KDLA, Frankfort
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-statehood Virginia land grants, surveys, and patents for Kentucky District. Virginia military bounty warrants (south of Green River). Kentucky was part of Virginia until 1 June 1792.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well preserved at LVA and KDLA

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Catlettsburg. Formed 1860 from Carter, Greenup, and Lawrence. Ashland (city) is the main population center — Ashland Independent city records. American Rolling Mill Company (ARMCO/AK Steel) records document industrial workers. Boyd County Historical Society. Ohio River town — river trade and steamboat records. Jesse Stuart Foundation literary archives. FamilySearch microfilm.
